headlines.title: US employers defy economic shock from Iran war and add a surprisingly strong 115,000 jobs in April
Federal court rules against new global tariffs Trump imposed after loss at the Supreme Court
US Fires on Iran Targets as Trump Demands Deal From Tehran
US stocks rise to records after a solid jobs report overshadows higher oil prices
Apple reaches chipmaking deal with Intel, pushing its stock to new record

notes: US equity markets reached new all-time highs on May 8, 2026, driven by stronger-than-expected April Non-Farm Payrolls (+115K) and a tech rally led by Apple and Intel.
Brent Crude Oil surged past $101 per barrel following direct military exchanges between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz.
A US federal trade court struck down a second round of unilateral 10% global tariffs imposed by President Trump, marking another setback for his trade policies.
